name: Morbidostat (demo speed)
description:
Morbidostat, demo speed: the same control loop as the 'Morbidostat' example compressed into ~25 minutes (25 cycles of 60 s) so it can be watched end-to-end in Studio.

The intervals are biologically meaningless - no culture grows measurably in 60 s - so this doc is for learning the block structure and watching the live chart, NOT for running an actual evolution experiment. Use the 'Morbidostat' example for that.

Because the OD trace is sampled every 3 s instead of every 1 min, the slope constant is 480 instead of 24: 2*3600/(n*dt) with n = 5 and dt = 3 s. The freshness window in each tube's guard is scaled by the same rule, and is the second pace-coupled constant in the doc: it must EXCEED this doc's growth phase - 10 samples x 3 s = 30 s, the age of this cycle's OLDEST sample when the decision is taken - and stay UNDER its cycle pace of 60 s, so that a sample from the PREVIOUS cycle can never satisfy it. 30 s < 45 s < 60 s, hence `last=45s` where the faithful doc has `last=11min` (10 min < 11 min < 12 min). Change either loop's `count` or `pace` and you must recompute BOTH constants.

The retry back-off is 1 s rather than 2 s, scaled with everything else. Be precise about what that buys: the FULL retry budget does not fit inside the 3 s sampling pace - three attempts cost 2 x 1 s of back-off plus three read latencies - but `pace` is a floor, not a deadline. An overrun WIDENS the spacing of the following sample; it never drops one. Here that costs nothing scientifically (these intervals are biologically meaningless anyway); on the faithful doc, whose sampling pace is 1 min against a 2 s back-off, the budget fits with room to spare. Everything else is identical.

Fault tolerance: this doc takes 750 measurements, and a live run of the demo-speed doc once died at cycle 17 on ONE flaky densitometer read. So every retry-safe device call inherits the workflow default retry (3 attempts, 1s apart). The pumps' `dispense` is deliberately NOT covered - `volume_ml` is relative, so a retried dispense would double-dose the culture; a default never retries a non-idempotent verb. One exception to 'every retry-safe call is retried', and it is worth knowing: `set_thermostat(enabled=true)` opens a thermal MODE that each densitometer then holds for the whole run, and the executor refuses a retry whose orphan-clearing `stop` would close an open mode (the densitometer's `stop` closes optics AND thermal). So a `measure` that fails with its job still TRACKED - a job timeout, or an exhausted poll budget - is not retried at all: it falls straight through to `on_error: continue` and costs its sample. The incident's actual fault (the job reports `failed`, which untracks it before the error surfaces) IS retried, which is the case this doc exists to survive.

The three OD reads additionally carry `on_error: continue`, so a read that fails all its attempts drops one sample instead of the run, and its two sibling lanes keep reading. Each tube's service branch is therefore guarded by `count(od_N, last=45s) > 0`, independently of its neighbours. That is a FRESHNESS guard, and the duration window is the load-bearing part of it. `Stream` is append-only, so the whole-stream form `count(od_N) > 0` is true FOREVER once a tube has read even once: a sensor that dies at cycle 10 would leave that guard standing while `last(od_N)` and both trailing means froze on the last successful trace. The branch condition becomes a CONSTANT and the same arm fires every remaining cycle with no feedback at all - and since a healthy vial is above OD_THR and out-growing its dilution by construction, the arm that latches is frequently DRUG. Each latched cycle applies the concentration recursion c_k = c_(k-1)*V/(V+dV) + C*dV/(V+dV), whose fixed point is C, the undiluted stock. That is an open-loop drug injector running on a dead sensor: it sterilizes the vial while the run still reports `completed`. `last=45s` is what lets the guard see a NEWLY dead sensor - it proves a sample landed during THIS cycle's growth phase, which no whole-stream predicate can.

So read the guard precisely: a tube that produced no reading THIS CYCLE is skipped entirely - no injection of either kind - and it stays skipped until it reads again; a tube that read at least once this cycle decides on the trace it has. Skipping withholds the dilution as well as the drug, which does perturb the culture - but far less than dosing a vial nobody is watching. The guard also categorically prevents a decision over an empty window, which is an EvaluationError and would kill the run. The blanks are NOT tolerated: a failed blank is a setup failure and must stop the run before any culture is committed. Nor is the thermostat setup, which asks for 6 attempts rather than 3 - but NOT for the reason first written here. The original argument was that a jitter-free back-off cannot de-synchronize three colliding writers, so the contending set would need three clean rounds to thin out (3 -> 2 -> 1) and 3 attempts would leave zero headroom. MEASURED ON REAL HARDWARE (2026-07-14, 25 trials), that is not what happens: the race fired 9 times and produced 10 `block_retried` events, and every single one was `attempt 1 of 6` - max observed retry depth 1. A collision leaves ONE loser at a time, and it wins on its very next attempt, because by then the other writers have already released the file. `attempts: 2` would have covered every observed case. Six stays because it is free headroom on a one-time block - it is headroom, not a computed requirement. The block is NOT tolerated either way: a silently-unset thermostat would be a scientific defect, not a dropped sample.

Caveat on a dropped sample: the slope estimator assumes evenly spaced samples, and a dropped one perturbs it. Note precisely HOW, because it is not what you would guess. `mean(od_N, last=10)` is a SAMPLE window - samples[-10:] over the append-only stream - so it always returns ten values. On a lossy cycle it therefore reaches back ACROSS THE CYCLE BOUNDARY and pulls in the previous cycle's final sample(s): taken BEFORE that cycle's injection, ~6-8% higher in OD, and a full cycle earlier. The span is LONGER than it looks, not shorter, and it straddles a dilution discontinuity. Simulated on the faithful doc against a true r of 0.8/h, one dropped sample biases r_est by -11.5% to +3.9% depending on where in the trace it falls (worst case -15.5%); three drops in one cycle, -12.6%. Bounded, and biased DOWNWARD - toward withholding drug, which is the conservative direction.

Why that is tolerable, stated honestly: NOT because the decision is a sign test - it is not. `480 * (mean(last=5) - mean(last=10)) / last(od) > r_dil` is a THRESHOLD test at r_dil, and by the controller's own design invariant (r_dil = r0/2) the culture is pinned AT that threshold in steady state, so its decisions are habitually marginal - exactly the regime in which a 15% bias can flip one. What makes it safe is the COST of a flip, not its rarity: a flipped decision costs one wrong 1 ml injection; dV is identical in both arms, so the dilution rate is untouched either way; and the bang-bang loop corrects itself on the next clean cycle. (Simulated: a tube riding out ~40 transient faults still lands at drug ~= 1.24 ~= IC50, the same place as the fault-free control.) Retry makes drops rare to begin with, and the run log records every one (`block_retried` / `block_error_tolerated`, and `tolerated_errors` in the report) - read them before you trust a cycle.

metadata.name: Morbidostat (demo speed)
metadata.author: lab-devices examples

body.label: tube {tube}: OD stuck high this cycle (contamination leg 1/2, freshness-guarded)
tube {tube}: contamination latch - drug maxed AND OD still high -> stays contaminated forever
tube {tube}: fire once, on the contamination edge
tube {tube}: latch the alarm so it never refires

compute.value: c_{tube} * working_volume_ml/(working_volume_ml + dose_ml) + drug_stock_x_mic * dose_ml/(working_volume_ml + dose_ml)

then.label: tube {tube}: growing above threshold -> drug, else medium
restore tube {tube} to V (over-draw; needle is at the V line)
tube {tube}: service (skipped entirely if this cycle produced NO reading - a dead sensor must never latch an open-loop injector - or while too dilute to read)

body.label: tube {tube}: service only while not contaminated (a contaminated tube receives no further drug or medium)

operator_input.name: od_min
operator_input.prompt: Minimum OD before any injection (algorithm OD_MIN). Below this the tube is too dilute to read reliably and gets no action at all.

operator_input.name: od_thr
operator_input.prompt: Drug-injection OD threshold (algorithm OD_THR). Drug is only ever injected above this OD.

operator_input.name: r_dil
operator_input.prompt: Dilution rate in 1/h (algorithm r_dil). The controller's setpoint: a tube growing faster than this is out-growing its dilution and gets drug. By design r_dil = r0/2, which pins the culture at its own IC50.

operator_input.name: dose_ml
operator_input.prompt: Volume added per injection in ml (algorithm dV). Both branches add the same volume, so the dilution rate does not depend on the decision - only the drug content does.

operator_input.name: drug_stock_x_mic
operator_input.prompt: Drug stock concentration as a multiple of MIC (Stock A = 10x MIC). Recorded for provenance only - it is never actuated on, but it is what you need to reconstruct the per-cycle concentration from the run log.

operator_input.name: blanks_ready
operator_input.prompt: Blank tubes (sterile medium, no cells) seated in all three holders? The blank slope is what every later OD reading is measured against.

children.label: thermostats to 30 C, all three at once; retry rides out the agent's duplicate-serial state-store race (see docs/experiment-engine-limitations.md), which failed 32% of parallel attempts when re-measured on real hardware 2026-07-14. 6 attempts is HEADROOM, not a computed requirement: measured over 25 trials, every retry this race ever needed was `attempt 1 of 6` - a collision leaves one loser, and it wins one second later - so 2 would have sufficed. The block is one-time, so the headroom costs nothing. Deliberately NOT tolerated: a silently-unset thermostat is a scientific defect. The finalizer switches them off again
blank each densitometer (every later OD is measured against this). Retried, but NOT tolerated: a blank that will not read is a setup failure, and it must stop the run before any culture is committed

operator_input.name: cultures_ready
operator_input.prompt: Culture tubes seated, lines primed, and ALL THREE valves physically parked at position 0 (all closed)? `home` only declares the rotor position - it does not move it - so a wrong answer here mis-addresses every tube.

children.label: medium_valve: never rotate across the 0<->N boundary
drug_valve: never rotate across the 0<->N boundary
waste_valve: never rotate across the 0<->N boundary
home + configure each valve (serial: `home` persists device state too)
culture working volume V in ml, held constant by the waste needle

body.label: tube {tube}: drug concentration starts at 0 (no drug in a fresh culture)
tube {tube}: contamination alarm not yet fired - alarm-latch seed
whole-run abort: operator flagged emergency_stop at setup

abort.message: all vials contaminated - nothing left to run

body.label: whole-run abort: every tube has been dropped from service
tube {tube} OD (retried; a read that still fails costs this one sample, not the run)
read all three tubes at once (independent densitometers); a lane that fails is absorbed in that lane, so its two siblings still read
growth phase: 10 evenly spaced OD samples -> the slope the decision uses
park every valve closed for the growth phase (no siphoning)
